target.title: [Repo Assist] test(connection): add coverage for DeviceTokenReceived bootstrap handoff
target.body:
🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

## Summary

Adds four unit tests covering the `HandleDeviceTokenReceived` path in `GatewayConnectionManager` — the bootstrap token handoff logic that was the subject of the recent pairing fix commits (#600, #601).

The `DeviceTokenReceived` event path had no test coverage despite encoding a critical security/correctness invariant: once the **node** role receives its device token, the bootstrap token should be cleared from the registry so it cannot be reused. The **operator** role receiving a device token must **not** clear the bootstrap, because the node still needs it for its role-upgrade pairing.

## New Tests

| Test | What it verifies |
|------|-----------------|
| `DeviceTokenReceived_NodeRole_ClearsBootstrapTokenFromRegistry` | Bootstrap token is erased after node gets device token |
| `DeviceTokenReceived_OperatorRole_PreservesBootstrapToken` | Bootstrap token is kept when operator gets device token |
| `DeviceTokenReceived_NodeRole_WhenBootstrapAlreadyNull_Succeeds` | No crash / exception when bootstrap is already null |
| `DeviceTokenReceived_WithIdentityStore_PersistsToken` | Token is forwarded to the `IDeviceIdentityStore` |

Also adds `SimulateDeviceTokenReceived` helper to `MockLifecycle` and `MockGatewayClient` using the same reflection pattern as the existing `SimulateHandshakeSucceeded`.

## Test Status

All 269 connection tests pass (4 new + 265 existing). All 870 tray tests pass. Infrastructure failure (GitVersion env-file write) is a pre-existing CI environment issue unrelated to these changes.
